rationale: Both candidates converge on the same grounded, actionable suggestion (derive scan targets from `_dit_modules` instead of a hardcoded tuple), which only tangentially overlaps the actual ground-truth concern (dsocek's request to drive naming from `_packed_modules_mapping`/`stacked_params_mapping` for fused-projection renames) — neither hits it squarely, so recall is low and equal. X adds a plausible, grounded second point (text-encoder LoRA silent no-op) and correctly calibrates its verdict to COMMENT, matching the actual outcome (two LGTM approvals). Y's round-trip-test suggestion is well-grounded and specific, but its output is oddly triplicated verbatim and its REQUEST CHANGES verdict contradicts both its own minor/nit severity tags and the noted 'gates clean, mergeable' status, undermining precision and trustworthiness.
